steps.title: Predict the isotope pattern
steps.content: The researchers concluded that M23 formed at 14.0°C and F02 formed at 20.8°C. The passage says colder water is deeper, and septa formed in deeper water contain more oxygen-18. If the conclusion is right, the colder M23 sample should contain more oxygen-18 than the warmer F02 sample. A weakening finding must reverse or disrupt that pattern.

steps.title: Find the direct contradiction
steps.content:
• Choice A - At a depth of 140 meters in the nautilids' habitat, the water is 20.8°C and the concentration of oxygen-18 is equal to that in sample F02.: this supports F02 forming at the warmer temperature.
  • Choice B - The concentration of oxygen-18 in F02 is higher than that in M23.: this reverses the expected pattern, because the colder M23 sample should have more oxygen-18.
  • Choice C - The concentration of oxygen-18 in F02 is lower than that in M23.: this matches the predicted pattern.
  • Choice D - At a depth of 385 meters in the nautilids' habitat, the water is 14.0°C and the concentration of oxygen-18 is equal to that in sample M23.: this supports M23 forming at the colder temperature.
The answer is B.
